excerpt: The victim roundup for the 12-day war states that 28 people were killed in Israel and that 27 of them were civilians.
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 104-107 in tool view
transform_note: Directly coded as civilian because the same source distinguishes the remaining death as an off-duty soldier.
confidence_note: High confidence for the aggregate civilian count, subject to the article's media-source status.

excerpt: The Beersheba strike report identifies Cpl. Eitan Zacks as an off-duty soldier and adds that apart from him, all those killed in the 12-day conflict were civilians.
pages_or_sections: title and main text; lines 69-80 and line 126 in tool view
transform_note: Kept in the military numerator under status-based coding. This is the key classification-sensitive claim in the bundle.
confidence_note: High confidence that one fatality had explicit soldier status; lower confidence on whether status-based coding is the preferred DR classification rule.

excerpt: The Israel Democracy Institute notes that with the launch of Operation Rising Lion, the defense minister declared a special home front situation, a legal measure used when the civilian population is likely to be attacked in all or part of Israel.
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 114-122 in tool view
transform_note: Used to justify statewide civilian exposure for the 12-day bombardment.
confidence_note: High confidence for the nationwide emergency-status interpretation.

excerpt: A Times of Israel report citing Israel's Central Bureau of Statistics says the country's population stood at almost 10.1 million in late April 2025.
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 90-93 in tool view
transform_note: Rounded to 10.1 million as the civilian-stock baseline before subtracting in-country military and temporary absence scenarios.
confidence_note: High confidence for an order-of-magnitude national population baseline.

excerpt: During the airport closure, Israel reported a plan to bring home more than 100,000 Israelis stranded abroad since the outbreak of hostilities.
pages_or_sections: main text; line 102 in tool view
transform_note: Used only as an upper-bound temporary-absence adjustment; the best estimate subtracts 50,000 rather than the full 100,000.
confidence_note: Medium confidence for bounding temporary absence; low confidence for any precise daily stock impact.

excerpt: An Al-Monitor report citing the IISS Military Balance gives Israel 169,500 active personnel and 465,000 reservists shortly before the June 2025 war.
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 79-81 in tool view
transform_note: The active-duty figure anchors all three military-exposure scenarios; the reserve figure is used only as a ceiling on plausibility, not as the deployed force.
confidence_note: Medium confidence because the source is secondary reporting of IISS figures.

excerpt: The IDF Chief of Staff said, after the war, that the military had called up 'tens of thousands of soldiers' and prepared across all borders.
pages_or_sections: main text; line 44 in tool view
transform_note: Grounds the reserve-call-up scenario of +20,000 / +40,000 / +60,000 personnel above the active-duty baseline.
confidence_note: High confidence for a large reserve call-up; low confidence for the exact scenario breakpoints.

excerpt: The IDF reported that Home Front Command forces, including both regulars and reservists, were deployed and operating at impact sites inside Israel.
pages_or_sections: title and main text; lines 28-36 in tool view
transform_note: Supports treating some reserve call-ups as directly exposed in-country personnel rather than remote administrative strength.
confidence_note: High confidence for the qualitative deployment pattern.
